summary: Moved src/melee/lb/lbdvd.c from NonMatching to Equivalent by replacing several nonmatching stubs and partial matches with C implementations for the preload/DVD cache path. The PR also tightened related prototypes, preload cache structs, static data initialization, and symbol boundaries, with small dependent fixes in gm and stage call sites. The decomp.dev bot reported 7 new matches and +2824 matched bytes, including full matches for lbDvd_80018F68, lbDvd_800187F4, lbDvd_80017960, lbDvd_80017740, lbDvd_80018CF4, lbDvd_80018C6C, and lbMemory_800150F0, plus near-complete improvements for lbDvd_80018254, lbDvd_80018A2C, and lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object("melee/lb/lbdvd.c") is classified as Equivalent instead of NonMatching.
Implemented or substantially rewrote multiple lbdvd preload/cache functions, replacing stubs such as lbDvd_80017960, lbDvd_80018254, lbDvd_800187F4, lbDvd_80018A2C, and lbDvd_80018F68.
Refactored lbDvd_80017740 from goto-heavy decompiler output into a structured loop using free_index and ARRAY_SIZE(preloadCache.entries), while preserving assert paths and preload entry behavior.
Reworked lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile to use the actual selected PreloadEntry, OSRoundUp32B(preloadEntry->size), sizeof(HSD_Archive), and clearer heap/archive/raw_data handling.
Added lbdvd helper inlines and stack-local table structs for preload setup, including inline1 for LbRb.dat and inline2 for EfMnData.dat, EfCoData.dat, ItCo., and IfAll.
Added lbDvd_80017960 logic for camera-mode cache, stage preload through Stage_802251B4, player character preload through Player_80031CB0, and Kirby-copy preload through Player_80031D2C.

Corrected lbdvd public prototypes: several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS signatures became void or typed parameters; lbDvd_80018A2C and lbDvd_80018C2C use u8 mask arguments; lbDvd_80017740 uses int/u8 parameter types.
Changed PreloadCacheScene typing in src/melee/lb/types.h: is_heap_persistent became bool[2], PreloadCacheSceneEntry.char_id became int, and game_cache.stage_id became InternalStageId.

Changed static preload defaults in lbdvd.static.h from a zeroed PreloadCache to a typed PreloadCacheScene initializer with MJ_COUNT, stage 0x148, CHKIND_NONE entries, and a nonzero default PreloadEntry template.
Adjusted callers to match new types: gm_801A3F48 and gm_801BF85C assign true/false to is_heap_persistent; gm_801B23F0 now returns void; Stage_802251B4 no longer takes an unused second s32 argument.

Reworked lbMemory_800150F0 into a structured while loop and replaced the old external filename/assert workaround with explicit OSReport("[LbMem] Error: lbMemFreeToHeap %x.\n", arg1) and __assert("lbmemory.c", 283, "0").

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt: lbDvd_80018F68 size increased from 0x198 to 0x2C8 and lbDvd_80019100 was removed as a separate symbol; former broad lbMemory data labels were split into individual lbl_803BA2C0/lbl_803BA2CC/etc. string/data labels.
smart_moves: Using ARRAY_SIZE(preloadCache.entries) instead of hard-coded 0x50/80 made preload-cache loops self-documenting and reduced the chance of struct-size drift breaking future matches.
Replacing magic allocations with OSRoundUp32B and sizeof(HSD_Archive) clarified intent while still supporting matching/equivalence.
Converting is_heap_persistent to bool and propagating true/false callsite changes aligned the struct model with actual usage and made preload state code easier to reason about.
Fixing neighboring API signatures in gm_801B23F0 and Stage_802251B4 removed bogus return/unused-argument pressure from lbdvd call sites.
Splitting default preload state into a typed PreloadCacheScene initializer exposed the actual shape of lbDvd_803BA638 instead of hiding it behind a larger zeroed PreloadCache.
Inline helper variants were used to isolate repeated preload-entry scans and stack-local table setup, a common tactic when small source-shape differences affect PowerPC codegen.
decomp_lessons: When a module is blocked by callsite codegen, inspect and correct adjacent function signatures; incorrect return types or unused parameters can force wrong call sequences outside the target file.
Struct field typing matters for equivalence: bool fields, enum-like stage IDs, and signed character IDs can change comparisons, stores, and argument passing.
Symbol-map boundaries should be revisited after matching: this PR merged the old lbDvd_80019100 range into lbDvd_80018F68 and split large lbMemory data objects into individual string labels.
Prefer semantic constants and macros only when they preserve codegen; here ARRAY_SIZE, OSRoundUp32B, and sizeof(HSD_Archive) improved readability and matched/equivalent output.

For preload/cache code, preserving interrupt disable/restore windows and state transitions is critical; lbDvd_80018254, lbDvd_800187F4, and lbDvd_80018A2C all explicitly bracket scans with OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts.
Default static data can be a matching lever: carefully initializing sentinel scene entries and PreloadEntry defaults can remove misleading broad zero-data symbols.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based names for unknown lbdvd functions, such as lbDvd_80017740, lbDvd_80018254, lbDvd_800187F4, lbDvd_80018A2C, and lbDvd_80018F68.
Used descriptive local names where evidence was clear: free_index, entry, other, game_cache, persistent_heap, load_score, load_state, effect_index, raw_data, archive.
Static inline helpers remained generic inline0/inline1/inline2/inline3/inline3_alt, signaling matching-oriented source shaping rather than claimed semantic names.
Data labels in symbols.txt use generic lbl_803BA2C0-style names for split strings/data when no better semantic name is evidenced.
Boolean persistent-heap fields are named is_heap_persistent and are assigned true/false at call sites.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted decompiler gotos in lbDvd_80017740 and lbMemory_800150F0 into structured loops while retaining matching-relevant control flow, asserts, and early returns.
Used static inline helper functions to encourage specific inlining/code layout for repeated preload-entry scans and table-based preload requests.
Used local stack arrays/structs for file preload descriptors, likely to match stack layout and load order around DVDConvertPathToEntrynum and lbDvd_80017740 calls.
Kept a '#pragma dont_inline on' region around lbDvd_800174E8, indicating codegen/inlining control remained relevant in this unit.
Adjusted casts away where struct fields were correctly typed, e.g. comparing entry->state, entry->load_state, and entry->entry_num directly instead of forced s8/s16/s32 casts.
Updated function-size and data-symbol boundaries in symbols.txt after the C source revealed a larger lbDvd_80018F68 function and individual lbMemory string literals.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched-code improvement for GALE01: total functions -1, matched code 43.56% (+0.07%, +2824 bytes).

Bot-reported new full matches: lbDvd_80018F68 (+712 bytes), lbDvd_800187F4 (+504), lbDvd_80017960 (+288), lbDvd_80018CF4 (+65), lbDvd_80017740 (+45), lbDvd_80018C6C (+32), and lbMemory_800150F0 (+13).
Bot-reported unmatched-function improvements: lbDvd_80018254 to 98.68%, lbDvd_80018A2C to 99.53%, and lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile to 98.38%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish lbDvd_80018254, lbDvd_80018A2C, or lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile from near-match to exact match.
Search for follow-up renames or struct refinements for PreloadCacheScene, GameCache, PreloadCacheSceneEntry.x5, and lbDvd_803B72C0_t.
Check later symbol-map changes around lbDvd_80018F68/lbDvd_80019100 to confirm the merged function boundary remained stable.
Look for additional lbdvd callsite signature cleanups involving lbDvd_80017740, Stage_802251B4, or gm_801B23F0.
Search for future PRs replacing inline0/inline1/inline2/inline3 helper names with semantic names once behavior was better understood.
